data.title: Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. (ESRT), a New York City-focused real estate investment trust (REIT), has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. ESRT's portfolio includes office, retail, and multifamily assets, with the Empire State Building and its world-famous Observatory as the flagship property.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues: $767.9 million, reflecting a 3.8% increase from the previous year driven by higher rental and observatory revenues.
  • Operating Income: $158.7 million, an 8.2% increase due to higher revenues and controlled operating expenses.
  • Net Income: $80.4 million, a slight decrease of 4.8% compared to the previous year, impacted by lower gains on property dispositions.
  • Net Income Attributable to Common Shareholders: $47.4 million, reflecting a 3.3% decrease from the prior year.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. (ESRT) is a NYC-focused real estate investment trust (REIT) that owns and operates a portfolio of office, retail, and multifamily assets. The company's flagship property is the Empire State Building, which includes the world-famous Observatory.
  • Portfolio Composition: As of December 31,¬Ý2024, ESRT's portfolio comprised approximately 7.8 million rentable square feet of office space, 0.8 million rentable square feet of retail space, and 732 residential units.
  • Leasing Activity: In 2024, ESRT signed a total of 1,324,824 rentable square feet of new, renewal, and expansion leases, contributing to a 130 basis point increase in Manhattan office occupancy to 89.0% and a 160 basis point increase in the leased rate to 94.2%.
  • Observatory Performance: The Empire State Building Observatory had approximately 2.6 million visitors in 2024, maintaining its status as the #1 Attraction in the World and the U.S. according to Tripadvisor‚Äôs 2024 Travelers‚Äô Choice Awards.
  • Sustainability Initiatives: ESRT is recognized as a leader in sustainability, focusing on net zero emissions, energy efficiency, and indoor environmental quality. The company has published the Empire Building Playbook to guide other buildings in achieving net zero carbon reduction.
  • Geographical Performance: The majority of ESRT's office properties are located in midtown Manhattan, with one property in Stamford, Connecticut. The company has disposed of non-core assets in the greater New York metropolitan area, including properties in Stamford, Norwalk, White Plains, and Harrison.
  • Future Outlook: ESRT intends to focus its acquisition strategy on NYC office, retail, and multifamily properties to achieve attractive returns on invested capital. The company also plans to enhance shareholder value through leasing, Observatory ticket sales, and sustainability goals.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company executed a consensual foreclosure of the First Stamford Place property, which was placed in receivership, and reclassified related debt. Additionally, the company closed on a $715 million unsecured credit agreement, consisting of a $620 million revolver and a $95 million term loan facility, maturing in 2029. The company also issued $225 million in senior unsecured notes, including Series I, J, and K Green Guaranteed Senior Notes, to support its strategic initiatives.
  • Capital Management: The company maintained a strong liquidity position with $385.5 million in cash and cash equivalents and $500 million available under its unsecured revolving credit facility. It declared dividends of $0.035 per share for each quarter of 2024, equating to an annualized rate of $0.14 per share. The Board authorized a $500 million stock and operating partnership unit repurchase program, with no repurchases made during 2024. The company also refinanced the Metro Center property mortgage, securing a $71.6 million interest-only loan with a four-year term.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to meet its short-term liquidity needs through cash flows from operations, cash on hand, and available borrowing capacity. For long-term capital requirements, it will rely on cash flows, credit facilities, mortgage financings, and potential equity issuances. The company aims to maintain its REIT status by distributing at least 90% of its taxable income and will continue to evaluate its dividend and capital allocation policies. The company is focused on leveraging its capital structure to support growth and acquisition opportunities while managing its debt obligations.

Challenges and Risks

  • Geographic Concentration: ESRT faces significant challenges due to its geographic concentration in New York City and Stamford, Connecticut. This concentration exposes the company to local economic and regulatory risks, such as business layoffs, industry slowdowns, and increased local taxes. The company's reliance on the Empire State Building and its Observatory for a substantial portion of revenue further heightens this risk.
  • Real Estate Market Risks: The real estate market presents challenges such as adverse economic and geopolitical conditions, which could reduce demand and rental rates for office, multifamily, and retail spaces. The COVID-19 pandemic has already impacted these areas, and future public health crises or economic downturns could further affect property demand and value. The shift towards remote work could also reduce demand for office space.
  • Tenant Financial Stability: ESRT faces risks related to tenant financial stability, as the bankruptcy or insolvency of tenants could lead to lease terminations and material losses. The competitive leasing environment in New York City may require the company to offer concessions or make significant capital expenditures to attract and retain tenants.
  • Ground Leases and Development Risks: The company is exposed to risks from its ground leases, which could result in loss of property use if leases are breached or not renewed. Development activities also pose risks, including construction delays and cost overruns.
  • Inflation and Debt Levels: Significant inflation could increase property and development costs, potentially reducing profit margins if rental rates cannot be raised accordingly. Additionally, ESRT's debt levels and associated restrictions could limit financial flexibility and impact cash flow.
  • Market Risks: ESRT is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations that could affect the cost of debt and refinancing capabilities. The company's reliance on external capital sources for growth, due to REIT distribution requirements, adds to this risk. Additionally, natural disasters and climate risks could impact property values and operations, particularly given the concentration of properties in the New York metropolitan area.

SEC Filing: Empire State Realty Trust, Inc. [ ESRT ] - 10-K - Feb. 28, 2025

data.title: ENTERPRISE FINANCIAL SERVICES CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Enterprise Financial Services Corp, a diversified financial services company, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report, detailing its financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces. The company, which operates primarily in Arizona, California, Florida, Kansas, Missouri, Nevada, and New Mexico, focuses on providing a broad range of business and personal banking services, including wealth management and specialized lending niches.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Interest Income: $851.1 million, reflecting an increase from $764.9 million in 2023 due to higher average loan balances and increased loan yields.
  • Total Interest Expense: $283.0 million, up from $202.3 million in 2023, primarily due to increased deposit interest expense.
  • Net Interest Income: $568.1 million, a slight increase from $562.6 million in 2023, driven by higher interest income offset by increased interest expense.
  • Net Income: $185.3 million, a decrease from $194.1 million in 2023, impacted by higher noninterest expenses and increased deposit costs.
  • Basic Earnings Per Share: $4.86, down from $5.09 in 2023, reflecting the decrease in net income.
  • Diluted Earnings Per Share: $4.83, compared to $5.07 in 2023, consistent with the decline in net income.
  • Return on Average Assets: 1.25%, down from 1.41% in 2023, indicating a decrease in profitability relative to asset size.
  • Return on Average Common Equity: 10.60%, a decrease from 12.27% in 2023, reflecting lower net income.
  • Net Interest Margin (fully tax equivalent): 4.16%, down from 4.43% in 2023, primarily due to higher interest expense on deposits.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company offers a broad range of business and personal banking services, including wealth management, commercial and industrial loans, commercial real estate, real estate construction and development, residential real estate, and specialty loans. The focus is on privately-held businesses, their owner families, and success-minded individuals.
  • Geographical Performance: Enterprise Financial Services Corp operates primarily in Arizona, California, Florida, Kansas, Missouri, Nevada, and New Mexico, with loan and deposit production offices throughout the United States. The company leverages its geographical presence to maximize growth opportunities.
  • Sales Units: The company has a diversified loan portfolio with significant contributions from commercial and industrial loans, commercial real estate loans, and construction and land development loans. The focus on specialized lending niches such as SBA 7(a) loans, life insurance premium finance, sponsor finance, and tax credit-related lending has contributed to loan growth.
  • New Product Launches: The company has developed expertise in niche markets, including SBA 7(a) loans, life insurance premium finance, sponsor finance, and tax credit-related lending, which differentiate it from competitors.
  • New Production Launches: In the fourth quarter of 2024, the company successfully completed the conversion of its legacy core system into a new core banking platform, enhancing operational efficiency and customer service capabilities.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue focusing on building long-term client relationships, expanding its specialized lending activities, and leveraging technology to enhance service delivery. It also aims to participate in future NMTC allocations and maintain disciplined growth through acquisitions.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Core System Conversion: In 2024, the company successfully completed the conversion of its legacy core system into a new core banking platform, which is expected to enhance operational efficiency and customer service capabilities.
  • Loan Origination: The company focused on originating high-quality commercial and industrial (C&I) loan relationships, leveraging specialized products such as sponsor finance, life insurance premium financing, and tax credit lending to expand and diversify its geographic concentration.
  • Capital Management: The company increased its tangible book value per share by 10.1% in 2024, driven by a return on average assets of 1.25%. It repurchased 626,778 of its common shares at a weighted-average share price of $46.95, and paid dividends of $1.06 per share, a 6% increase from the previous year. The company maintained strong capital ratios, with a common equity tier 1 capital ratio of 11.8% and a total capital to risk-weighted assets ratio of 14.6%. The company also had a revolving line of credit for an aggregate amount up to $25 million, all of which was available at the end of 2024.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue its focus on strategic loan growth and capital management, with an emphasis on maintaining strong capital ratios and liquidity. It aims to leverage its new core banking platform to enhance customer service and operational efficiency. The company also intends to explore further opportunities for capital investment, particularly in areas that align with its strategic focus on high-quality C&I loan relationships and specialized financial products.

Challenges and Risks

  • Competitive Market: The company operates in highly competitive markets, facing competition from large financial institutions and non-bank entities, which could impact its ability to attract and retain customers. The company's growth strategy relies on acquisitions, which present integration risks and potential exposure to unknown liabilities.
  • Economic and Market Conditions: The company faces significant risks related to general economic and market conditions, including potential adverse effects from economic downturns, changes in monetary and fiscal policies, and trade policy uncertainties. The banking industry has experienced volatility due to high-profile bank failures, which could erode customer confidence and impact liquidity and stock prices.
  • Regulatory Risks: Regulatory risks include changes in government regulation, compliance with the Bank Secrecy Act, and potential sanctions for non-compliance with fair lending laws.
  • Financial Risks: Financial risks involve interest rate fluctuations, credit losses, and impairments to goodwill. The company also faces credit and liquidity risks due to concentration in certain markets and reliance on brokered deposits.
  • Technological and Cybersecurity Risks: Technological and cybersecurity risks are significant, with potential operational disruptions and reputational damage from cyber-attacks.
  • Management Strategies: Management has identified the need to maintain asset quality and manage expenses as critical to sustaining growth and profitability. The company is focused on building long-term client relationships and leveraging specialized lending niches to differentiate itself in the market. Strategic acquisitions are a key component of the company's growth strategy, but they require careful integration to achieve expected benefits.
  • Market Risk Management: The company is exposed to interest rate risk, which could affect its net interest margin and financial performance. It uses derivative transactions to manage interest rate risk, but these transactions introduce additional credit and market risks. The company must carefully monitor these risks to mitigate potential adverse effects on its business and financial condition.

SEC Filing: ENTERPRISE FINANCIAL SERVICES CORP [ EFSC ] - 10-K - Feb. 28, 2025

data.title: AM Best expects Vantage‚Äôs 2024 combined ratio to ‚Äútrack close to breakeven‚Äù
data.description:
(The Insurer) - Vantage Risk in the past two years has achieved operating profitability while its combined ratio is expected to improve to near breakeven for 2024, AM Best has commented while affirming the Bermudian (re)insurer‚Äôs A- (Excellent) rating.

AM Best also affirmed Vantage‚Äôs long-term issuer credit ratings of ‚Äúa-‚Äù (Excellent).

All ratings apply to Bermuda-based Vantage Risk Ltd and its Wilmington, Delaware-based affiliates Vantage Risk Specialty Insurance Company and Vantage Risk Assurance Company, and have stable outlooks.

The rating affirmations follow an active initial four years of operations for the Greg Hendrick-led group, AM Best, noted.

‚ÄúIn the first two years, underwriting income was impacted adversely by elevated catastrophe losses; however, as Vantage Group has continued to grow to scale in 2023 and 2024 and moved property catastrophe business off its balance sheet through its insurance-linked securities vehicle, AdVantage Retro I Ltd, its underwriting results have improved,‚Äù it said.

Vantage‚Äôs strategy is expected to provide the group with accretive income while continuing to reduce volatility in its operating results in future years, AM Best said.

‚ÄúOver the past two years, the group has achieved operating profitability though the group has yet to achieve a full-year combined ratio below 100. AM Best expects full-year combined ratios for 2024 to continue to benefit from these changes and track close to breakeven,‚Äù it continued.

Vantage continues to expand its primary insurance operations, the rating agency continued, noting a slow start in 2021 due to various operational challenges.

The company had initially hired industry veteran Jack Kuhn as the CEO of its insurance business but he departed to set up Westfield Specialty after only eight months. Former Axa XL executive Alex Blanco was appointed to the role in November 2021.

‚ÄúAs the group continues to execute its business plans, the reinsurance portfolio has become relatively smaller than the primary portfolio; and thus, has less of an impact to its bottom line,‚Äù AM Best said.

Vantage‚Äôs diversification and capabilities have expanded significantly since launch, although AM Best highlighted that some level of operational risks remains as new business seasons.

‚ÄúAM Best will continue to monitor the group‚Äôs progression as it enters its fifth year of operation. AM Best anticipates that Vantage Group‚Äôs operating performance will continue to improve as its business plan is executed,‚Äù it said.

data.title: ROSEN, A LEADING INVESTOR RIGHTS LAW FIRM, Encourages TELUS International (Cda) Inc. Investors to Secure Counsel Before Important Deadline in Securities Class Action ‚Äì TIXT
data.description:
NEW YORK, Feb. 28,¬Ý2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) ‚Äî

WHY:¬ÝRosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, reminds purchasers of securities of TELUS International (Cda) Inc. between February 16,¬Ý2023 and August 1,¬Ý2024, both dates inclusive (the ‚ÄúClass Period‚Äù), of the important March 31,¬Ý2025 lead plaintiff deadline.

SO WHAT: If you purchased TELUS International securities during the Class Period you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

WHAT TO DO NEXT: To join the TELUS International class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=34482 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than March 31,¬Ý2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party acting on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation.

WHY ROSEN LAW: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Many of these firms do not actually litigate securities class actions, but are merely middlemen that refer clients or partner with law firms that actually litigate the cases. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm achieved the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company at the time. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs‚Äô Bar. Many of the firm‚Äôs attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.

DETAILS OF THE CASE: According to the lawsuit, defendants, throughout the Class Period, failed to disclose to investors that: (1) TELUS International‚Äôs AI Data Solutions offerings required the cannibalization of its higher-margin offerings; (2) TELUS International‚Äôs declining profitability was tied to TELUS International‚Äôs drive to develop AI capabilities; (3) TELUS International‚Äôs shift toward AI put greater pressure on the company‚Äôs margins than previously disclosed; and (4) as a result of the foregoing, defendants‚Äô positive statements about TELUS International‚Äôs business, operations, and prospects were materially misleading and/or lacked a reasonable basis. When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.

To join the TELUS International class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=34482 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action. A class action lawsuit has already been filed.

No Class Has Been Certified. Until a class is certified, you are not represented by counsel unless you retain one. You may select counsel of your choice. You may also remain an absent class member and do nothing at this point. An investor‚Äôs ability to share in any potential future recovery is not dependent upon serving as lead plaintiff.

Follow us for updates on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-rosen-law-firm, on Twitter: https://twitter.com/rosen_firm or on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/rosenlawfirm/.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

-------------------------------

Contact Information:

¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý¬ÝPhillip Kim, Esq.

data.title: UniCredit S.p.A. - PRESS RELEASE
data.description:
KOMISJA NADZORU FINANSOWEGO

Raport bie≈ºƒÖcy nr 21 / 2025

Data sporzƒÖdzenia: 2025-02-28

Skr√≥cona nazwa emitenta

UniCredit

Temat

PRESS RELEASE

Podstawa prawna

Inne uregulowania

Tre≈õƒá raportu:

VOLUNTARY PUBLIC EXCHANGE OFFER LAUNCHED BY UNICREDIT S.P.A. ON ALL ORDINARY SHARES OF BANCO BPM S

P.A.

PRESS RELEASE

Milan, 28 February 2025 - With reference to the voluntary public exchange offer pursuant to and for

the purposes of art. 102 and 106, paragraph 4, of the TUF (the "Offer"), concerning all the ordinary

shares of Banco BPM S.p.A. ("BPM"), UniCredit S.p.A.(‚ÄúUniCredit‚Äù) informs that today it has

filed with Consob for approval the prospectus prepared pursuant to Regulation (EU) 1129/2017,

relating to the UniCredit shares to be assigned to the shareholders of BPM who shall adhere to the

Offer.

This press release is disclosed pursuant to art. 36 of the Issuers‚Äô Regulation.

* * *

THIS PRESS RELEASE DOES NOT CONSTITUTE THE EXTENSION OF AN OFFER TO ACQUIRE, PURCHASE, SUBSCRIBE

FOR, SELL OR EXCHANGE (OR THE SOLICITATION OF AN OFFER TO ACQUIRE, PURCHASE, SUBSCRIBE FOR, SELL OR

EXCHANGE), ANY SECURITIES IN ANY JURISDICTION, INCLUDING THE UNITED STATES OF AMERICA, AUSTRALIA,

CANADA, JAPAN, OR ANY OTHER JURISDICTION WHERE TO DO SO WOULD CONSTITUTE A VIOLATION OF THE LAWS OF

SUCH JURISDICTION AND ANY SUCH OFFER (OR SOLICITATION) MAY NOT BE EXTENDED IN ANY SUCH JURISDICTION.

The public voluntary exchange offer described in this press release (the ‚ÄúOffer‚Äù) will be

promoted by UniCredit S.p.A. (the ‚ÄúOfferor‚Äù or ‚ÄúUniCredit‚Äù) over the totality of the

ordinary shares of Banco BPM S.p.A. (‚ÄúBPM‚Äù).

This press release does not constitute an offer to buy or sell BPM‚Äôs shares.

The Offer will be launched in Italy and will be made on a non-discriminatory basis and on equal

terms to all shareholders of Banco BPM S.p.A.. The Offer will be promoted in Italy as BPM‚Äôs shares

are listed on the Euronext Milan organised and managed by Borsa Italiana S.p.A. and, except for what

is indicated below, is subject to the obligations and procedural requirements provided for by

Italian law.

The Offer is not as of today being made in the United States (or will not be directed at U.S.

Persons, as defined in Regulation S under the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, as subsequently amended

the ‚ÄúU.S. Securities Act‚Äù)), Canada, Japan, Australia or any other jurisdiction where to do so

would constitute a violation of the laws of such jurisdiction and any such offer (or solicitation)

may not be extended in any such jurisdiction (‚ÄúOther Countries‚Äù). The Offeror reserves the right

to extend the Offer in the United States exclusively to certain professional investors who qualify

as Qualified Institutional Buyers, as defined in Rule 144A under the U.S. Securities Act, by way of

a private placement in compliance with United States federal laws and regulations concerning the

offer of financial instruments and with United States laws concerning tender offers, insofar as

applicable. Such potential extension of the Offer in the United States would occur by way of a

separate offer document restricted to Qualified Institutional Buyers.

A copy of any document that the Offeror will issue in relation to the Offer, or portions thereof,

is not and shall not be sent, nor in any way transmitted, or otherwise distributed, directly or

indirectly, in the Other Countries. Anyone receiving such documents shall not distribute, forward or

send them (neither by postal service nor by using national or international instruments of

communication or commerce) in the Other Countries.

Any tender in the Offer resulting from solicitation carried out in violation of the above

restrictions will not be accepted.

This press release and any other document issued by the Offeror in relation to the Offer do not

constitute and are not part neither of an offer to buy or exchange, nor of a solicitation to offer

to sell or exchange financial instruments in the United States or in theOther Countries. Financial

instruments cannot be offered or sold in the United States unless they have been registered pursuant

to the U.S. Securities Act, or are exempt from registration. Financial instruments offered in the

context of the transaction described in this press release will not be registered pursuant to the U

S. Securities Act, and UniCredit does not intend to carry out a public offer of such financial

instruments in the United States. No financial instrument can be offered or transferred inthe Other

Countries without specific approval in compliance with the relevant provisions applicable in such

countries or without exemption from such provisions.

This press release may only be accessed in or from the United Kingdom who are ‚Äúqualified

investors‚Äù within the meaning of Article 2(e) of Regulation (EU) 2017/1129 as forming part of

United Kingdom law by virtue of 'European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018, asamended, and who (i) have

professional experience in investment matters under section 19(5) of the Financial Services and

Markets Act 2000 (Financial Promotion) Order 2005 (as amended, the ‚ÄúDecree‚Äù); or (ii) are

persons who have a high net worth and who fall within article 49(2)(a) -(d) of the Decree (the

aforementioned subjects, jointly, the ‚ÄúRelevant Persons‚Äù). Any investment activity to which this

document refers is available only to Relevant Persons.

Financial Instruments described in this press release are made available only to Relevant Persons

and any solicitation, offer, agreement to subscribe, purchase or otherwise acquire such financial

instruments will be directed exclusively at such persons). Any person who is not a Relevant Person

should not act or rely on this document or any of its contents.

Tendering in the Offer by persons residing in jurisdictions other than Italy may be subject to

specific obligations or restrictions imposed by applicable legal or regulatory provisions of such

jurisdictions. Recipients of the Offer are solely responsible for complying with such laws and,

therefore, before tendering in the Offer, they are responsible for determining whether such laws

exist and are applicable by relying on their own advisors. The Offeror does not accept any liability

for any violation by any person of any of the above restrictions.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 5 PM ET
data.description:
Inflation Eased. Why It Isn't a Sigh of Relief for the Fed or Consumers.

Easing price pressures weren't enough to assuage shoppers, who slowed their spending last month.

S&P 500, Dow Pare Gains After Trump-Zelensky Meeting

U.S. rallied late in the session, but still notched losses for the month, with the Nasdaq Composite's 4% decline leading the retreat.

Trump-Zelensky Meeting Implodes, Threatening Peace Talks

The Ukrainian leader abruptly left the White House after a combative Oval Office exchange over how to address the threat of Russian aggression.

U.S. trade deficit balloons to record high as businesses race to avoid tariffs

The trade sector seems set to be a drag on headline GDP growth in the first quarter, but it is hard to estimate how much.

The Tariff Pain Is Getting Real for Chinese Companies

The Trump administration is set to slap an additional 10% tariff on Chinese imports, sparking panic among some manufacturers.

Canada GDP Growth Accelerates to 2.6% in Fourth Quarter

Canada's economy perked up in the final months of last year as consumers continued to ramp up their spending, spurred on by the central bank's rapid-fire interest-rate cuts.

Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: Focus on U.S. Jobs, ECB Decision

Key U.S. jobs data, a likely interest-rate cut by the ECB and China's annual National People's Congress will all attract attention during the week.

French Inflation Cools to Lowest in Four Years as ECB Prepares to Cut Rates Again

The European Central Bank looks primed to cut its key interest rate next week, after French inflation fell, and price increases held steady in Germany, Italy and Spain.

India's Economy Picks Up Speed

India's economy picked up in the most recent quarter as manufacturing and government spending regained momentum.

Drug Stocks Are the New Safe Bet in a Shaky Market

After two years in the shadows, health stocks are back as a haven for investors navigating economic uncertainty.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 5 PM ET
data.description:
Intel Delays $28 Billion Ohio Semiconductor Project

The site in New Albany, Ohio, is now set to be completed in 2030, with operations starting as late as 2031, compared with the original operations start date that was set for 2025.

Microsoft to Close Skype, Shift Users to Teams

The tech giant is hanging up on the communication service in May.

Super Micro to Build Third California Campus

Super Micro Computer is building a third campus set to be 3 million square feet in California's Silicon Valley.

Samsung, BBC, Others Respond to U.K. Competition Watchdog's Google Search Investigation

The watchdog has published a raft of comments from companies, lobbyists and academics as part of its investigation into Google under its new tech antitrust rules.

BASF Expects Earnings Growth on Cost Cuts

The chemical giant anticipates a slight earnings increase this year due to cost savings that will offset continued investment in China and a tough economic environment.

Allianz Lifts Dividend, Launches Buyback After Rise in Profit

The insurer proposed a dividend increase of 12% for 2024 after net profit rose in the final quarter.

Valeo Shares Plunge After Carmakers Cancel, Delay Orders

The auto-parts supplier said its customers had canceled orders worth billions of dollars and delayed new ones in response to economic and technological uncertainty.

British Airways Owner IAG to Buy Back Shares After Profit Rise

The group said it would buy back around $1 billion in shares after earnings were boosted by commercial and cargo demand.

Videogaming's All-In Bet: Can Two Titans Lift a $58 Billion Industry?

A new Nintendo Switch console and a "Grand Theft Auto" sequel spell a big year ahead for games, but there might be only a few winners.

This Company Gets 98% of Its Money From the U.S. Government. DOGE Is Coming for Firms Like It.

The Trump administration is looking to cut federal contracts. Few companies stand as exposed as Booz Allen Hamilton.

data.title: American Healthcare REIT, Inc. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
American Healthcare REIT, Inc., a prominent player in the healthcare real estate sector, has released its 2024¬Ý10-K report, showcasing significant financial and operational achievements. The report highlights the company's strategic initiatives, business performance, and the challenges it faces in the evolving healthcare industry.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenues and Grant Income: $2,070.7 million, reflecting an increase from the previous year primarily due to higher resident fees and services revenue.
  • Net Loss: $(35.6) million, an improvement from the previous year's net loss, driven by increased revenues and cost management.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $(0.29), compared to $(1.08) in the prior year, indicating a reduction in loss per share due to improved financial performance.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates through four reportable business segments: integrated senior health campuses, outpatient medical (OM) buildings, senior housing operating properties (SHOP), and triple-net leased properties. Each segment contributes to the overall revenue with specific focus areas such as resident fees and services for RIDEA properties and rent from leased properties.
  • Geographical Performance: The company operates clinical healthcare properties throughout the United States, the United Kingdom, and the Isle of Man, indicating a diverse geographical presence. This international footprint allows the company to leverage different market dynamics and regulatory environments.
  • Sales Units: As of December 31,¬Ý2024, the company owned and/or operated 314 buildings and integrated senior health campuses, representing approximately 19,155,000 square feet of gross leasable area. This reflects the company's extensive operational scale in the healthcare real estate sector.
  • New Product Launches: The company expanded its integrated senior health campuses segment by $82,361,000 through acquisitions, development, and expansion of campuses, indicating a strategic focus on growth in this area.
  • New Production Launches: The company acquired $138,839,000 of senior housing facilities during 2024, which are included in the SHOP segment, showcasing an increase in operational capacity and service offerings.
  • Future Outlook: The company aims to grow earnings and cash flows, maintain financial flexibility, increase portfolio value, and generate attractive risk-adjusted returns. This includes external growth through acquisitions, selective development of senior health campuses, and positioning the balance sheet for growth.
  • Operational Strategy: The company utilizes a RIDEA structure for its integrated senior health campuses and SHOP, allowing participation in operational performance upside while bearing associated risks. This strategic approach is designed to enhance revenue potential and operational efficiency.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: American Healthcare REIT, Inc. undertook several strategic initiatives in 2024, including the transition of certain leased senior housing and skilled nursing facilities to a managed portfolio utilizing a RIDEA structure. This transition is part of a broader strategy to enhance operational efficiency and increase revenue from resident fees and services. The company also expanded its portfolio by acquiring 14 senior housing properties in Oregon and five in Washington, as well as one in Georgia, to strengthen its presence in these markets.
  • Capital Management: In 2024, the company focused on capital management through multiple public offerings and an at-the-market equity offering program. It raised $772.8 million from the February 2024 Offering and $471.2 million from the September 2024 Offering. Additionally, the ATM Offering generated $120.2 million in gross proceeds. These funds were primarily used to pay down variable-rate mortgage loans and lines of credit, significantly reducing interest expenses. The company also redeemed OP units in exchange for common stock, increasing its ownership in the operating partnership to 98.7%. Furthermore, the company maintained a quarterly distribution of $0.25 per share, reflecting an annualized rate of $1.00 per share.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, American Healthcare REIT, Inc. plans to continue its focus on strategic acquisitions and transitions to managed portfolios to drive growth. The company aims to leverage its increased liquidity from recent equity offerings to further reduce debt and explore new investment opportunities. It also intends to maintain its REIT status by distributing at least 90% of its taxable income to shareholders, ensuring compliance with federal tax requirements. The company is committed to enhancing shareholder value through disciplined capital management and strategic expansion.

Challenges and Risks

  • Dependency on Major Tenants and Operators: The company faces significant risks related to its dependency on major tenants and operators, particularly the financial deterioration or insolvency of these parties, which could materially and adversely affect the company's financial condition. The concentration of properties in specific geographic areas, such as Indiana and Ohio, magnifies the impact of regional economic downturns or natural disasters.
  • Regulatory Risks: The healthcare industry is subject to extensive regulation, and changes in laws or regulations could adversely affect the company's tenants' ability to make rent payments. The company also faces risks from reimbursement rates from third-party payors, including Medicare and Medicaid, which may not keep pace with inflation, potentially impacting tenants' operations and profitability.
  • Operational Risks: Operational risks include the reliance on Trilogy Management Services, LLC, which manages a significant portion of the company's revenue-generating properties. Any adverse developments in Trilogy's business could have a material adverse effect on the company. Furthermore, the company is exposed to cybersecurity threats, which could disrupt operations and damage its reputation.
  • Economic and Market Risks: Management highlights the challenges posed by economic slowdowns, which could lead to tenant defaults and reduced property values. The company has experienced net losses in the past and may continue to face financial challenges due to depreciation, interest expenses, and general administrative costs. The company is also focused on managing inflationary pressures, which could increase operating expenses and interest costs.
  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to market risks, including interest rate fluctuations that could increase borrowing costs. The company's variable-rate debt is subject to interest rate changes, which could materially impact financial results. Additionally, the company faces foreign currency exchange risks due to its investments in the UK and the Isle of Man, which could affect financial performance due to exchange rate fluctuations.

SEC Filing: American Healthcare REIT, Inc. [ AHR ] - 10-K - Feb. 28, 2025

data.title: Blackstone's Schwarzman Had Another $1 Billion Year ‚Äî WSJ
data.description:
By Miriam Gottfried

It's good to be Steve. Blackstone CEO Steve Schwarzman took home just over $1 billion in compensation and dividends in 2024, according to the firm's annual financial filing.

As in prior years, the bulk of that, some $916 million, came from dividends on Schwarzman's roughly 20% stake in Blackstone.

The private-equity titan, whose net worth Forbes pegs at $48.2 billion, also received $84 million from Blackstone. Virtually all of that was carried interest tied to the performance of Blackstone's investments.

Schwarzman is paid a $350,000 salary.

Blackstone pays out all of its distributable earnings to shareholders. Those were up 18% in 2024, helping Schwarzman's haul come in 11.5% higher than in 2023. His total earnings still fell short of the record $1.2 billion he got in 2022.

Blackstone's stock posted strong performance in 2024, returning 35% including dividends, compared with a 25% total return for the S&P 500. The firm's market capitalization of $197 billion now ranks it among the 50 biggest U.S. companies by that metric.
